_meta.description: Add 11 missing phase2_flag_types referenced in Session A v8 specification but absent from the database.
_meta.reason: Session A v8 specification defines 25 phase2 flag codes. Only 14 exist in phase2_flag_types. The 11 missing flags are required for correct annotation of high-frequency terms, semantic breadth, eschatological usage, wisdom literature concentration, relational direction, volitional component, cross-testament shift, Aramaic forms, multi-registry anchors, divine-human parallels, and metaphor roots. These must be present before Phase 2 patches for joy (097) and subsequent registries can reference them by id.

rows.description: Term occurs 200 or more times; high frequency makes it a primary anchor for its semantic field
Term covers 4 or more distinct semantic domains; breadth warrants attention in Session B cross-part synthesis
Term appears as a cross-reference in 3 or more registries; functions as a conceptual anchor across multiple word families
God and humans appear as subject of this term in structurally parallel contexts within the verse set
Term is predominant in eschatological or apocalyptic passages; eschatological register warrants attention in Session B
Term is predominant in Proverbs, Job, or Ecclesiastes; wisdom literature concentration warrants attention in Session B
Term meaning is grounded in concrete physical or sensory metaphor rather than direct abstract description
Term is inherently directed toward another person, group, or God; relational orientation is intrinsic to its meaning
Term carries a dimension of will, choice, or intention alongside or within its inner-state meaning
Term exhibits a meaningful semantic shift between OT and NT usage; shift warrants attention in Session B
Term is an Aramaic form of a Hebrew root occurring in Daniel, Ezra, or post-exilic contexts
